reasoning: The 30-year Treasury yield recently hit 4.92% on August 15, 2025, representing a short-term local peak after rising from around 4.80% earlier in the month. The yield has largely oscillated between 4.50% and 4.97% over the past year. Because 4.92% sits near the top of its recent historical range, the probability of the yield being strictly higher on any given date in the near term is below 50% due to expected mean reversion and market expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts beginning in September. However, large federal debt issuance and term-premium pressures from persistent inflation and proposed tariffs provide a floor for long-term yields. Consequently, I forecast a lower probability of an increase in the immediate short term (1 week and 1 month) with my uncertainty increasing (drifting closer to a 50/50 coinflip) for the 3-month and 6-month horizons.
